channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Rubio details U.S. demands as Iran peace talks falter
item.dc:creator: Noah Robertson, John Hudson
item.description: The secretary of state is testifying publicly to Congress for the first time since the war began, as the regional conflict worsens and lawmakers’ patience runs thin.

item.title: Russia hammers Ukrainian cities, killing at least 22
item.dc:creator: Serhii Korolchuk, David Stern
item.description: Moscow has intensified aerial bombing of Ukrainian cities as its battlefield advances have stalled.

item.title: Kenyan court extends order blocking U.S. hospital for American Ebola patients
item.dc:creator: Rael Ombuor
item.description: The court extended for seven days its order stopping the opening of the U.S. field hospital. It also directed the Kenyan government to detail its deal with the U.S.

item.title: Israel says it is holding off on striking Beirut after U.S. request
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix, Susannah George
item.description: Tehran suspended ceasefire negotiations because of Israel’s escalating military attacks in Lebanon and U.S. strikes, an Iranian official told The Post.

item.title: As Russia fails to achieve war aims in Ukraine, Putin needs a way out
item.dc:creator: Catherine Belton
item.description: European officials say Moscow's escalating aggression is a result of increasing difficulties that Russia is facing militarily and economically.

item.title: Hardening views on migrants push Europe toward Trump-like tactics
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola, Ellen Francis
item.description: New rules were driven by high numbers of asylum seekers from “safe countries” and the fatigue of voters after waves of migration, European officials say.

item.title: Kenyans protest U.S. plan for Ebola field hospital to treat Americans
item.dc:creator: Rael Ombuor
item.description: Angry residents, who said their lives were in danger, set fires and clashed with police as they protested the facility intended to treat Americans.
